name: Against Malaria Foundation
mission: We help protect people from malaria We fund long-lasting insecticidal nets LLINs and work with distribution partners to ensure they are used. We track and report on net use and impact.
theoryOfChange: The Against Malaria Foundation (AMF) outlines its strategic approach through its 'Our Process' and 'What we do' sections on its website. This framework details how AMF identifies funding gaps for long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs), partners with in-country organizations for distribution, and monitors net usage and malaria case rates to assess impact. The process includes pre-distribution activities to establish household net needs, independent supervision during distribution, and post-distribution check-ups (PDCUs) via household visits to ensure nets are used and maintained. AMF emphasizes accountability, transparency, and demonstrating impact as core principles, tracking metrics such as nets distributed, people protected, lives saved, and cases of malaria averted.
scoreSummary: Against Malaria Foundation (US) shows moderate impact and moderate alignment.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization achieves a 44/100 score due to its world-class financial efficiency and transparency, balanced against a lower alignment score for donors seeking specific religious or cultural focus.
areas_for_improvement: Limited direct alignment with specific religious or cultural giving frameworks beyond general humanitarian aid [6]
Impact score reflects a focus on capacity building and distribution logistics rather than direct medical intervention [3]

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated at 20/50, reflecting a low donor fit for those seeking specific religious pathways, though the cause remains urgent [6]. As a sadaqah-eligible organization, it serves as a highly efficient vehicle for general voluntary charity aimed at saving lives [6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates moderate impact with a score of 24/50, focusing on the large-scale distribution of LLINs to reduce hospital admissions and malaria cases [4][5]. While it maintains a high Charity Navigator score of 94.0/100, the impact model is categorized as capacity building through the provision of physical goods [1][3].

strengths: Maintains a perfect 100.0% program expense ratio, ensuring all funds go directly to malaria prevention [1]
Exceptional fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised, indicating that overhead costs are covered by specific private donors [1][2]
High transparency regarding net distribution and post-distribution monitoring [4][5]
baseline_narrative.summary: The Against Malaria Foundation (US) focuses on funding and distributing long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) to combat malaria in high-risk regions [3]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $119,395,632 while maintaining a program expense ratio of 100.0% [1][2].

rich_narrative.headline: The Against Malaria Foundation (US) provides life-saving insecticidal nets to protect vulnerable populations from malaria with exceptional financial efficiency.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want to ensure 100% of their contribution goes directly to life-saving supplies.
donor_motivations: Want to see 100% of their donation used for programs
Value rigorous evidence and data-driven impact
Prefer simple, highly effective health interventions
Care about malaria prevention in high-burden regions
giving_considerations: Public donations only buy nets; overhead is funded separately
The organization focuses on a single intervention (LLINs)
Financial reserves for operations are kept minimal
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking zakat-verified organizations or those who prefer supporting broad-based community development over single-issue health interventions.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade A reflects verified outcome tracking, external evaluations, and clear impact metrics.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation funds LLINs and partners with local health authorities to distribute them, followed by monitoring to ensure usage and track malaria reduction.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that net distributions actually lead to a reduction in malaria-related mortality.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Malaria remains a leading cause of death, and the foundation has the capacity to scale distributions with more funding.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="4">100.0% program expense guarantee</cite> for public donations.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="4">100.0% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring maximum resource allocation to the field.
The foundation holds a <cite id="10">94/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite> and has earned a <cite id="11">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
Impact is tracked through <cite id="12">external evaluation documents</cite> and specific metrics like <cite id="13">hospital admissions due to malaria</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Against Malaria Foundation's operational model is built on radical transparency and data-driven distribution. By focusing on a single, highly effective intervention—LLINs—the charity achieves <cite id="14">universal coverage in target regions</cite>. Their accountability is reinforced by <cite id="15">independent board members making up 100% of the board</cite>, which oversees a lean structure where <cite id="16">all public contributions buy nets</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Against Malaria Foundation (US) is a highly specialized humanitarian organization dedicated to <cite id="1">funding and providing long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs)</cite> to protect populations at high risk of malaria. Since its <cite id="2">founding in 2004</cite>, the organization has established a reputation for extreme financial efficiency, reporting <cite id="3">$62,286,888 in total revenue</cite> for the 2024 tax year. A defining characteristic of the foundation is its commitment to a <cite id="4">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that every dollar from public donations is used exclusively to purchase nets. This is made possible because <cite id="5">private donors cover all overhead and fundraising costs</cite>, resulting in a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>. The foundation operates primarily in <cite id="7">Africa, Asia, and South America</cite>, with significant distribution agreements in countries such as <cite id="8">Guinea, Nigeria, and the Democratic Republic of Congo</cite>. Beyond procurement, the organization maintains <cite id="9">rigorous post-distribution monitoring</cite> through net-use surveys and malaria case data tracking to ensure long-term impact and universal coverage.
